description: A cinephile who trusts the AI review more than the rating. Clicks a top pick and expects a rendered review panel with visible sections (headings + paragraphs), not a flat unformatted blob.

asserts.expression: var p=document.querySelector('.top-picks .event-card.is-open .event-panel'); if(!p) return false; var text=p.textContent.trim(); window.__reviewLen=text.length; return text.length >= 200;

llm.system_prompt: You are the review-reader. Your goals: understand whether to attend a top pick. Critique this Austin cultural events calendar site through your lens. Identify anything blocking your primary user story in <=150 words. Before claiming a selector is missing, cross-check the Ground truth JSON block — it reports per-selector DOM presence/count observed on the same page as the screenshot. If Ground truth says a selector exists, treat that as authoritative and do not contradict it without explicit screenshot evidence. Start with PASS or FAIL.
llm.goals: understand whether to attend a top pick; trust the ground-truth JSON over any impression that a selector is missing
